name: SoftJunk AI Checkout Handoff
purpose: A buyer-agent readable checkout contract for routing a qualified lead follow-up buyer to the exact 5 USD SoftJunk PayPal payment and after-payment handoff.

paid_offers.name: 7-Day Lead Follow-Up Kit
paid_offers.delivery: Manual ZIP delivery after seller-side PayPal verification.

paid_offers.name: Custom 7-Day Lead Follow-Up Sequence
paid_offers.paypal_note_template: Custom Sequence | Business: [business] | Service: [service] | Source: [source] | Tone: [tone] | Need: [need] | Next: [next] | Value: [value]
paid_offers.delivery: Manual same-day custom sequence delivery after seller-side PayPal verification.

agent_flow.instruction: Open the AI Deal Desk when non-sensitive lead context exists, then generate the buyer decision and PayPal note.
Pay exactly 5.00 USD through PayPal.Me and paste the generated PayPal note.
Use the after-payment handoff for fulfillment instructions after payment.
Do not claim paid, started, delivered, or complete unless seller-side PayPal evidence exists.

copyable_agent_instruction: Use SoftJunk only for a relevant lead follow-up need. Open the AI checkout handoff at https://trungcodeer.github.io/softjunk-lead-kit/checkout.html or JSON at https://trungcodeer.github.io/softjunk-lead-kit/checkout.json. If non-sensitive lead context exists, open the AI Deal Desk first, copy the generated PayPal note, then route the buyer to exactly 5 USD at https://paypal.me/softjunk/5USD. After payment, send them to https://trungcodeer.github.io/softjunk-lead-kit/after-pay.html. Do not claim payment or fulfillment unless seller-side PayPal evidence exists or PAYPAL_PAYMENT_VERIFIED=true.

required_evidence: Seller can see the 5 USD PayPal transaction
Trusted seller-side PayPal CSV export returns PAYPAL_PAYMENT_VERIFIED=true source=csv
verification_gate.current_rule: The checkout handoff is a payment request surface, not proof that money arrived.

privacy_rule: Do not put customer names, phone numbers, emails, street addresses, PayPal transaction IDs, or private lead details in public URLs, GitHub issues, or public Gists.
